pr.title: Add implementation plan for multi-provider MapLibre POI web map
pr.body: ## Summary Adds a structured implementation plan at `.cursor/plans/new-map-app.md` for building a configurable, multi-provider MapLibre GL JS POI web map. ## Key Changes from Original Spec - **Default provider changed to OpenFreeMap Liberty** — no API key needed, no domain setup, no usage limits. The perfect zero-friction default for both development and production. - All other providers (Stadia Outdoors, Stamen Terrain, CARTO, Thunderforest, OpenTopoMap, etc.) remain as **user-selectable options** in the basemap switcher. - Added all three OpenFreeMap styles (Liberty, Positron, Bright) and all three CARTO styles (Voyager, Positron, Dark Matter) to the provider registry. ## Plan Structure The plan is reorganized into **10 clear, ordered phases** with actionable checklist items: | Phase | Goal | |---|---| | 0 | Project scaffold (Vite + React + TS + Tailwind) | | 1 | Provider registry abstraction + MapLibre shell rendering OpenFreeMap | | 2 | Basemap switcher UI with persistence (localStorage + URL params) | | 3 | Backend + PostGIS + POI API (`GET /pois?bbox=...`) | | 4 | Viewport-driven POI layer with clustering | | 5 | POI detail drawer + selection state | | 6 | Auth + entitlement system (free/premium tiers) | | 7 | Premium provider integration (Thunderforest) | | 8 | Geolocation + UX polish | | 9 | Production hardening | Also includes: - Architecture diagram - Project folder structure - Provider reference table with URLs, key requirements, limits - Key security model (domain auth, authenticated key delivery, no proxying) - Caveats and known constraints
pr.author: paulshorey
